description: Hand-labelled sentence<->entity pairs for tuning the extract MATCH_THRESHOLD (issue #17). Each pair is an incoming observation sentence and an existing entity; should_match is whether the sentence should ENRICH that entity (attach as a new fact) under per-fact matching. Includes deliberate semantic traps (Endurance the server vs the film, taxman the project vs literal taxes, Dr Brand vs brand strategy) where context, not surface words, must decide. Embed with the real all-MiniLM-L6-v2 model; the mock embedder is non-semantic and useless here.

facts: The home server that runs the TARS agent
Runs nightly memory extraction on a cron

pairs.note: directly about the nightly extraction cron fact

facts: Vector database used for memory storage

pairs.note: should enrich, but MiniLM scores it ~0.15 (versioning/restart reads as a different aspect than 'runs in a Docker container') — below the top negative, so unseparable by a global gate. Flagged hard so the guard test does not enforce it.
